question.text: What happens to a call option's premium when the spot price increases (other factors constant)?

answer.text: Call premium first increases then decreases

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When spot increases, a call option becomes more valuable (closer to or deeper ITM). Spot ↑ → Call ↑, Put ↓. **The Trap:** The OPPOSITE happens for puts — put premium decreases when spot increases. **Cross-Exam Context:** Spot-premium relationship is tested in all options exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Think of it logically: if you have the right to BUY at a fixed price (call), higher market prices make that right more valuable.
